GET /accounts/{account_id}/billing/usage.description: Retrieve billing usage analytics for an account. Returns time-series data for all billable product metrics including Stream, Media (Images), Rate Limiting, Load Balancing, Argo, Workers, Workers KV, Image Resizing, and Spectrum.

parameters.description: Standard Cloudflare hex account identifier. The API gateway translates this to an internal numeric ID before forwarding to the backend service.
parameters.name: account_id

schema.description: Cloudflare resource identifier.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of metrics to include in the response. Available metrics depend on the endpoint. Billing usage supports: streamMinutesViewed, rateLimitingRequestsAllowed, loadBalancingQueries, argoAcceleratedBytes, workersRequests, workersKVReads, imageResizingRequests, spectrumBytesTransferred, mediaUniqueTransformations. Stream/media usage supports: streamMinutesViewed.
parameters.name: metrics

parameters.description: Start of the time range for the query (inclusive). ISO 8601 timestamp. Defaults to 6 hours before the current time.
parameters.name: since

parameters.description: End of the time range for the query (inclusive). ISO 8601 timestamp. Defaults to the current time.
parameters.name: until

parameters.description: Time unit to aggregate usage observations into. Data retention is approximately 18 months. The effective number of data points returned depends on the time range and granularity selected. For example, requesting hourly granularity over 18 months could produce up to ~13,000 data points; use the limit parameter to cap results and be aware that responses may be truncated.
parameters.name: time_delta

parameters.description: Maximum number of data points to return. The actual number of results depends on the interaction between the time range (since/until) and time_delta granularity. Results are truncated to this limit without error if the time range produces more data points than the limit allows.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Filter expressions to apply to the query. Format: field==value. Multiple filters can be combined.
parameters.name: filters

schema.description: Standard Cloudflare v4 API response containing billing usage analytics data.

errors.description: List of errors, empty on success.

result.description: Array of billing usage data points for the requested time range.

items.description: A single billing usage data point for a time period, containing metrics for all billable products.

argoAcceleratedBytes.description: Number of Argo accelerated bytes in this time period.

imageResizingRequests.description: Number of Image Resizing requests in this time period.

loadBalancingQueries.description: Number of Load Balancing DNS queries in this time period.

mediaUniqueTransformations.description: Number of Media unique image transformations in this time period.

rateLimitingRequestsAllowed.description: Number of Rate Limiting requests allowed in this time period.

spectrumBytesTransferred.description: Number of Spectrum bytes transferred in this time period.

streamMinutesViewed.description: Number of Stream billable minutes viewed in this time period.

ts.description: Unix timestamp (epoch seconds) for the start of this time period.

workersKVReads.description: Number of Workers KV reads in this time period.

workersRequests.description: Number of Workers requests in this time period.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

schema.description: Standard Cloudflare v4 API error response.

result.description: Empty array or null on error.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

400.description: Bad request - invalid parameters or query.

schema.description: Standard Cloudflare v4 API error response.

result.description: Empty array or null on error.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

schema.description: Standard Cloudflare v4 API error response.

result.description: Empty array or null on error.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

403.description: Insufficient permissions to access this resource.

GET /accounts/{account_id}/media/usage.description: Retrieve Media usage analytics for an account. This endpoint shares the same backend handler as the Stream usage endpoint and returns identical Stream metrics (streamMinutesViewed). The gateway rewrites this path to the shared usage handler.

parameters.name: account_id

schema.description: Cloudflare resource identifier.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of metrics to include in the response. Available metrics depend on the endpoint. Billing usage supports: streamMinutesViewed, rateLimitingRequestsAllowed, loadBalancingQueries, argoAcceleratedBytes, workersRequests, workersKVReads, imageResizingRequests, spectrumBytesTransferred, mediaUniqueTransformations. Stream/media usage supports: streamMinutesViewed.

parameters.description: Start of the time range for the query (inclusive). ISO 8601 timestamp. Defaults to 6 hours before the current time.

parameters.description: End of the time range for the query (inclusive). ISO 8601 timestamp. Defaults to the current time.
parameters.name: until

parameters.description: Time unit to aggregate usage observations into. Data retention is approximately 18 months. The effective number of data points returned depends on the time range and granularity selected. For example, requesting hourly granularity over 18 months could produce up to ~13,000 data points; use the limit parameter to cap results and be aware that responses may be truncated.
parameters.name: time_delta

parameters.description: Maximum number of data points to return. The actual number of results depends on the interaction between the time range (since/until) and time_delta granularity. Results are truncated to this limit without error if the time range produces more data points than the limit allows.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Filter expressions to apply to the query. Format: field==value. Multiple filters can be combined.
parameters.name: filters

schema.description: Standard Cloudflare v4 API response containing Stream usage analytics data.

errors.description: List of errors, empty on success.

result.description: Array of usage data points for the requested time range.

items.description: A single Stream usage data point for a time period.

streamMinutesViewed.description: Number of Stream billable minutes viewed in this time period.

ts.description: Unix timestamp (epoch seconds) for the start of this time period.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

schema.description: Standard Cloudflare v4 API error response.

result.description: Empty array or null on error.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

400.description: Bad request - invalid parameters or query.

schema.description: Standard Cloudflare v4 API error response.

result.description: Empty array or null on error.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

schema.description: Standard Cloudflare v4 API error response.

result.description: Empty array or null on error.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

GET /zones/{zone_id}/media/usage.description: Retrieve Media usage analytics for a zone. This endpoint shares the same backend handler as the Stream usage endpoint and returns identical Stream metrics (streamMinutesViewed). The gateway resolves the zone to its owning account and rewrites this path to the shared usage handler.

parameters.description: Standard Cloudflare hex zone identifier. The API gateway resolves this to the owning account and translates it to an internal numeric ID before forwarding to the backend service.
parameters.name: zone_id

schema.description: Cloudflare resource identifier.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of metrics to include in the response. Available metrics depend on the endpoint. Billing usage supports: streamMinutesViewed, rateLimitingRequestsAllowed, loadBalancingQueries, argoAcceleratedBytes, workersRequests, workersKVReads, imageResizingRequests, spectrumBytesTransferred, mediaUniqueTransformations. Stream/media usage supports: streamMinutesViewed.

parameters.description: End of the time range for the query (inclusive). ISO 8601 timestamp. Defaults to the current time.
parameters.name: until

parameters.description: Time unit to aggregate usage observations into. Data retention is approximately 18 months. The effective number of data points returned depends on the time range and granularity selected. For example, requesting hourly granularity over 18 months could produce up to ~13,000 data points; use the limit parameter to cap results and be aware that responses may be truncated.
parameters.name: time_delta

parameters.description: Maximum number of data points to return. The actual number of results depends on the interaction between the time range (since/until) and time_delta granularity. Results are truncated to this limit without error if the time range produces more data points than the limit allows.
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Filter expressions to apply to the query. Format: field==value. Multiple filters can be combined.
parameters.name: filters

schema.description: Standard Cloudflare v4 API response containing Stream usage analytics data.

errors.description: List of errors, empty on success.

result.description: Array of usage data points for the requested time range.

items.description: A single Stream usage data point for a time period.

streamMinutesViewed.description: Number of Stream billable minutes viewed in this time period.

ts.description: Unix timestamp (epoch seconds) for the start of this time period.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

schema.description: Standard Cloudflare v4 API error response.

result.description: Empty array or null on error.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
